title: `build`: Change of `--secret` does not trigger layer rebuild
body:
**Description**

When building a `Containerfile` that uses secrets with flag `--layers` set, change of secret does not trigger rebuild of a layer.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create `Containerfile` with contents:
   ```Containerfile
   FROM docker://docker.io/debian:11-slim
   RUN --mount=type=secret,id=secret cp /run/secrets/secret /
   ```
3. ```bash
   buildah build --layers --secret id=secret,src=<(echo "123") -f Containerfile .
   ```
4. ```bash
   buildah build --layers --secret id=secret,src=<(echo "321") -f Containerfile .
   ```

**Describe the results you received:**

First build pulled the debian image from dockerhub and built all layers:
```
STEP 1/2: FROM docker://docker.io/debian:11-slim
Trying to pull docker.io/library/debian:11-slim...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f03b40093957 done  
Copying config f8e203ddac done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=secret cp /run/secrets/secret /
COMMIT
--> b35e83b96c7b
b35e83b96c7b91863104d44e7d50e7acee99589e4a693cfbcf8e9a2b3d30e442
```

Second build used cached layers for all steps:
```
STEP 1/2: FROM docker://docker.io/debian:11-slim
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=secret cp /run/secrets/secret /
--> Using cache b35e83b96c7b91863104d44e7d50e7acee99589e4a693cfbcf8e9a2b3d30e442
--> b35e83b96c7b
b35e83b96c7b91863104d44e7d50e7acee99589e4a693cfbcf8e9a2b3d30e442
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Second step would be rebuilt, because contents of `/run/secrets/secret` file are changed.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
$ pacman -Q buildah
buildah 1.30.0-1
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.30.0
Go Version:      go1.20.3
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.1.0-rc.1
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.25.0
Git Commit:      04965f1198104afafd22197a479bd15c9b43148e
Built:           Wed Apr 19 10:50:43 2023
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Arch Linux releaseDISTRIB_ID="Artix"
DISTRIB_RELEASE="rolling"
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Artix Linux"
NAME="Artix Linux"
PRETTY_NAME="Artix Linux"
ID=artix
BUILD_ID=rolling
ANSI_COLOR="0;36"
HOME_URL="https://www.artixlinux.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://wiki.artixlinux.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://forum.artixlinux.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.artixlinux.org/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://terms.artixlinux.org/docs/privacy-policy/"
LOGO=artixlinux-logo
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux RijenkiiBook 6.3.1-artix1-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 04 May 2023 01:47:06 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```toml
# comments removed
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = []
pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "false", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev"
[storage.options.thinpool]
```

comments.body: Hi @rijenkii , thanks for creating the issue, are you sure if docker does it ? because buildah is unware of secrets contents,  secret is only mounted it is not stored with the image.

Checked -- yeah, surprisingly docker does not do that either, but the argument for why makes sense.

Is filesystem state used when computing layer hash?
Because if it is, then maybe there is a way to always run steps with secrets, then checking if layer hash is the same as before, and then using next steps from cache as if previous step was also taken from cache?

If there is truly nothing that can be done about this, then I guess I will just build containers without `--layers` and this issue can be closed.

@rijenkii Logic would be the `secret` never becomes part of the actual layer, it is only mounted for the build process and it is removed as soon as the build is completed there is a difference between mounting file and actually copying it inside the build container.

You can make it work it you manually copy secret to some otherfile inside the build using `echo $SECRET > mysecretfile`.

> Logic would be the secret never becomes part of the actual layer

...yeah, that makes a lot of sense now that I think about it.
However I guess documenting this would be nice.
@rijenkii I think documenting that would be good idea, do you want to create a PR ?
